comments.text: This problem is usually referred to as the Erdős-Lovász Tihany Conjecture. For an up-to-date overview, see Song's survey "The Erdős-Lovász Tihany Conjecture -- a survey'' (PDF). The survey records many additional positive cases beyond the ones already listed on this page. For example, this conjecture holds for every graph \(G\) with independence number \(\alpha(G)\ge 3\) that has no hole of length \(\ell\) with \(4\le \ell \le 2\alpha(G)-1\); see (arXiv:1805.11437) for this result. A recent progress not covered in the survey is due to Longbrake and Tariq (arXiv:2406.15164). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
